title: geum calthifolium subsp. nipponicum
intro: Geum calthifolium subsp. nipponicum, also known as Japanese avens, is a perennial plant species native to East Asia, characterized by its clusters of small, white to pink flowers and feathery foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Geum calthifolium subsp. nipponicum has been reported to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its potential health benefits.
